title: Italy/Switzerland 1993: via Ogbourne St George, Portsmouth, St Gotthard, Lugano, Cremona, Parma

main_route.excerpt: We set off from home at 2.40. ... The Ogbourne St. George **** was very pretty, big open chalk fields, hills, at a distance, all roun[d]
Hungerford. We wandered around antique shops, Simon looking for dining room chairs (unsuccessful) + Eve looking for hair pins (successful)
and on for lunch + the night to David + Maxines + Imogen 3½ + Iona 2 at Newbury
left for Hattie + James at Chandlers Ford. We had a delicious roast lunch, + chatted.
We drove to Portsmouth + ate in the carpark, surrounded by hoardes of French students.
We drove on into Surrey & camped near Leatherhead. Horsley *** the site was very wet, boggy grass & a lake, but had a shop & was friendly.
We drove on to London & Thomas found money for the metre for the van parked in Finsbury Circus
We had a tiring, [?ighty] drive home.

stops.excerpt: Simon drove all of us + Hattie + Katrina, Laura + Edward to see __Winchester__ __Cathedral__. It was lovely to see all the

excursions.label: Visit to Portsmouth Historic Dockyard (HMS Victory and Mary Rose)

stops.normalized_name: HMS Victory, Portsmouth Historic Dockyard, UK
stops.excerpt: The HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship was fascinating.

stops.normalized_name: Mary Rose Museum, Portsmouth Historic Dockyard, UK
stops.excerpt: Then we looked at the Mary Rose being sprayed, a wax which will impregnate the wood.

Then we set off for __Romsey Rapids__.
	The Rapids £11-20 for 1 adult,
5 children, were great fun.

unresolved.issue: Visited on April 17th on the way from Ogbourne St George to Newbury; no location given for Di and Ian's house
unresolved.excerpt: We visited Di + Ian. She opened the door with "Hello! Would you like a drink + a pee?"

unresolved.issue: Uncertain place name associated with David + Maxine's House, Newbury; could be a house/estate name

unresolved.original_wording: Portsmouth ferry crossing to France / Cherbourg
unresolved.issue: The previous extraction inferred a Portsmouth->Cherbourg ferry crossing and a French arrival port, but no ferry, crossing or French port is mentioned anywhere in the surviving diary pages. The Portsmouth entry describes only a sightseeing visit to the Historic Dockyard ('ate in the carpark'), and the narrative continues seamlessly to a Surrey campsite and a drive home with no Channel crossing. The crossing and Cherbourg anchor cannot be cited and have been moved here for review.

Named only in the trip title_guess, not in any transcribed diary page in this spread (A_0086-A_0090). The surviving pages document a UK-only outward and return loop (16-20 April); the Swiss/Italian transit through the St Gotthard Pass is undocumented here. Moved to unresolved as it cannot be cited.
Named only in the trip title_guess, not in any transcribed diary page in this spread. The Swiss overnight base at Lugano is undocumented in the surviving pages and cannot be cited.
Named only in the trip title_guess, not in any transcribed diary page in this spread. The Italian overnight base at Cremona is undocumented in the surviving pages and cannot be cited.
Named only in the trip title_guess, not in any transcribed diary page in this spread. The Italian overnight base at Parma is undocumented in the surviving pages and cannot be cited.

summary: The trip title records an Easter 1993 camping journey to Italy and Switzerland (via the St Gotthard Pass, Lugano, Cremona and Parma), but the surviving transcribed pages for this spread (A_0086-A_0090) document only a UK loop from 16-20 April: south from Birmingham via a campsite at Ogbourne St George, then antique-shopping in Hungerford and stays at family/friends in Newbury and Chandlers Ford, with day visits to Winchester Cathedral, Romsey Rapids and Portsmouth Historic Dockyard (HMS Victory and the Mary Rose). The return leg runs through a Surrey campsite near Leatherhead/Horsley and a stop in London (Finsbury Circus) before a tiring drive home.
extraction_notes: Corrected for citation compliance: every non-home main_route stop and every excursion stop now carries a real source_page_id and a verbatim supporting excerpt. The previously inferred Portsmouth->Cherbourg ferry crossing (and the Cherbourg crossing_terminal) had no support in the pages -- Portsmouth is described purely as a Historic Dockyard sightseeing visit and the narrative runs straight on to a Surrey campsite and home with no Channel crossing -- so the crossing was removed (crossings is now empty) and both it and the Cherbourg anchor were moved to unresolved. Portsmouth's role was changed from crossing_terminal to main_stop accordingly. The four continental anchors (St Gotthard, Lugano, Cremona, Parma) appear only in the title_guess and in no diary page, so they were moved to unresolved rather than kept uncited. The countries array retains FR/CH/IT to reflect the trip's stated destination even though only GB is documented in this spread. date_end (1993-04-20) reflects the last dated diary entry in this spread; the true end of the full Italy/Switzerland portion is unknown from these pages, which appear to cover only the opening UK leg of the trip.
